Global Volume vs. Price/Mix Contribution.text: Growth is healthy and broadening. FY2025 was price-led (+4% price/mix on ~flat volume); Q1 2026 re-accelerated to +10% organic with unit-case volume back to +3% and concentrate +8% — a higher-quality, margin-accretive mix.

items: Coca-Cola Zero Sugar grew +13% across every geographic segment (Q1 2026) — premiumization tailwind.
Portfolio pruned from ~400 to ~200 brands since 2020; innovation success rate now ~3× vs 2019.

Regional Pricing & Growth Drivers.text: Pricing power is strong in developed markets but emerging markets lean on affordability — and bottler input-cost inflation (aluminum) is not fully passed through, a margin watch-item.

items: Pricing actions were the primary price/mix driver in NA, EMEA and Latin America.
Q1 2026 aluminum costs 'outpaced pricing actions' at Coca-Cola Consolidated — incomplete inflation pass-through.
Packaging mix flexed (cans ↔ PET / returnable glass) to defend affordability against tariffs and input costs.
Asia Pacific price/mix turned negative on deliberate affordability initiatives to hold share.

Bottling System Structure & Re-franchisi.text: Asset-light, concentrate-led model. 59% of revenue is high-margin concentrate; the capital-intensive Bottling Investments segment is shrinking to ~5% of revenue after the CCBA divestment — de-risking the balance sheet.

items: Concentrate ops (FY25 rev.)
Finished-product / bottling ops (FY25 rev.)
Bottling Investments post-CCBA
~5% of net revenue

Bottling System Structure & Re-franchisi.title: Re-franchising milestones
rows: 41.52% of 66.52% to Coca-Cola HBC; option on remaining 25%

items: Global concentrate sales +8% in Q1 2026 — 5 points ahead of unit-case volume (extra shipping days / timing).

Franchise Partner Performance & Economic.text: Lead US bottler Coca-Cola Consolidated (COKE) is healthy but margins are easing (13.35%→12.9%) and leverage is notable (~2.3× net debt/EBITDA), on steady ~$300m/yr capex. Franchise economics are opaque — priced via an undisclosed incidence-based agreement.

items: FY2025 capex concentrated in US capacity: $90m Columbus OH campus, $35m Twinsburg OH can line, $15m Monroe NC ops center.

Category Portfolio Performance & Mix.text: Dominant category leader with durable share. Sparkling is ~69% of volume, global NARTD share is >40%, and the company has gained value share for years — while still/functional categories (water, sports, coffee, tea) now drive incremental growth.

items: Sparkling % of unit-case volume (2025)
Global NARTD value share
Global CSD share (Jan 2026)
Gained NARTD value share in Q1 2026 and FY2025; bottler Coca-Cola HBC +80 bps — its 6th consecutive year of share gains.
Recent growth led by Trademark Coca-Cola plus water, sports, coffee and tea.
Bottler (Coca-Cola Consolidated) sparkling bottle/can volume +16.7% in Q1 2026.

Innovation & Brand Health.text: Elite, but watch execution. #7 global brand at $60.1bn — though value slipped -1.9% YoY, and the incoming CEO calls innovation success 'not where it needs to be' despite a ~3× improvement vs 2019.

items: Interbrand 2025 rank
#7 global
Brand value (2025)
Innovation success vs 2019
Coca-Cola Zero Sugar +13% across all geographic segments — standout innovation/premiumization signal.
Incoming CEO Henrique Braun (Feb 2026): innovation success 'not where it needs to be' — must 'get closer to the consumer.'
Streamlined portfolio (~400 → ~200 brands) underpins the disciplined-innovation strategy.

Digital Strategy & D2C Penetration.text: Aggressive, credible digital shift. Digital is now >65% of media spend (from <30% in 2019) and >60% of customers are digitized, backed by a new Chief Digital Officer and ML demand planning — though pure D2C remains small and not separately disclosed.

Digital Strategy & D2C Penetration.title: Digital media mix (% of total media spend)

items: Customers digitized (Mar 2026)
New Chief Digital Officer (Sedef Salingan Sahin) effective Mar 2026 — unifying digital, data and operations.
ML-based Customer Demand & Supply Planning system driving a 'factory of the future' model.
Costa-led finished-product operations were 42% of revenue (2023, down from 49% in 2017).

Connected Vending & Marketing Tech ROI.text: Scaled connected-asset and martech edge. ~700k connected vending machines (Japan) and the 'Redeem' campaign doubled the all-time redemption record with strong Gen-Z lift — concrete evidence digital is monetizing, not just spending.
